form_description: Common App personal essay and typical supplemental essay prompts for college applications

sections.name: personal_essay
sections.title: Personal Essay (Common App)
sections.description: Your main essay - 650 words max, choose one prompt

sections.name: activities
sections.title: Activities & Experiences
sections.description: Brief descriptions of your extracurriculars (150 chars each)

sections.name: supplemental
sections.title: Supplemental Essays
sections.description: School-specific essays - these vary by school

questions.question_text: Some students have a background, identity, interest, or talent that is so meaningful they believe their application would be incomplete without it. If this sounds like you, then please share your story.
questions.helper_text: This is about what makes YOU unique. Don't write what you think they want to hear. Be authentic. Show, don't tell.

questions.question_text: The lessons we take from obstacles we encounter can be fundamental to later success. Recount a time when you faced a challenge, setback, or failure. How did it affect you, and what did you learn from the experience?
questions.helper_text: Focus on the growth and learning, not the obstacle itself. Admissions wants to see resilience and self-awareness.

questions.question_text: Reflect on a time when you questioned or challenged a belief or idea. What prompted your thinking? What was the outcome?
questions.helper_text: This can be intellectual, ethical, or personal. Show your capacity for critical thinking and growth.

questions.question_text: Reflect on something that someone has done for you that has made you happy or thankful in a surprising way. How has this gratitude affected or motivated you?
questions.helper_text: This reveals your values and relationships. Be specific about the act and genuine about its impact.

questions.question_text: Discuss an accomplishment, event, or realization that sparked a period of personal growth and a new understanding of yourself or others.
questions.helper_text: The 'spark' matters less than the growth. What changed in how you see yourself or the world?

questions.question_text: Describe a topic, idea, or concept you find so engaging that it makes you lose all track of time. Why does it captivate you? What or who do you turn to when you want to learn more?
questions.helper_text: Show your intellectual curiosity and how you pursue interests. Be specific about what fascinates you.

questions.question_text: Share an essay on any topic of your choice. It can be one you've already written, one that responds to a different prompt, or one of your own design.
questions.helper_text: Total freedom - what do you want admissions to know about you that doesn't fit elsewhere?

questions.question_text: Activity 1: Describe your most meaningful extracurricular activity and your role/accomplishments.
questions.helper_text: 150 characters. Use action verbs. Quantify impact if possible (led team of 12, raised $5K, etc.)
150 characters. What did you DO, not just participate in?
150 characters. Show breadth of interests or depth in one area.

questions.question_text: Why are you interested in [University Name]? What about our school appeals to you? (Why Us essay)
questions.helper_text: Be SPECIFIC. Name professors, programs, courses, traditions. Show you've researched. Explain fit, not just prestige.

questions.question_text: Why do you want to study [Major/Field]? What experiences led to this interest?
questions.helper_text: Connect your past experiences to future goals. Show genuine curiosity, not just career planning.

questions.question_text: Describe a community you belong to. What is your role and contribution?
questions.helper_text: Community can be family, school, neighborhood, online, cultural. Show how you contribute, not just belong.

questions.question_text: What would you contribute to our campus community?
questions.helper_text: Be specific. What clubs would you join? What perspectives do you bring? How would you engage?

questions.question_text: Tell us about a time you had a positive impact on others.
questions.helper_text: Focus on the impact, not the award or recognition. How did it change things for someone else?

questions.question_text: Is there anything else you want us to know? Use this space to explain any circumstances that affected your academic record or activities.
questions.helper_text: Optional but valuable if you have context to share: illness, family circumstances, school changes, etc. Be factual, not excuse-making.

questions.question_text: List any additional activities, awards, or experiences not captured elsewhere.
questions.helper_text: Work experience, family responsibilities, self-taught skills, hobbies that don't fit the activities list.

business_directions.name: STEM Focus
business_directions.description: Emphasize math, science, research, and technical projects in your essays and activities.

business_directions.name: Humanities Focus
business_directions.description: Emphasize writing, arts, social sciences, and community involvement.

business_directions.name: Balanced/Undecided
business_directions.description: Show breadth of interests and intellectual curiosity across disciplines.
